questions.q: Which Philippine island is famous for its underground river and pristine natural landscapes?
What is the name of the highest mountain peak in the Philippines?
In what year did the Philippines gain complete independence from the United States?
What is balut, a popular delicacy in the Philippines?
choices: A rice cake wrapped in leaves

questions.q: Which explorer arrived in the Philippines in 1521 and led the first European expedition to the islands?
Who was the Philippine national hero that led the Cry of Pugad Lawin during the Philippine Revolution?
What is the oldest city in the Philippines?
Which walled district in Manila is known as the historic heart of the city?
In what year did Gloria Diaz win the Miss Universe title?
Who was the second President of the Philippines, succeeding Emilio Aguinaldo?
Which Philippine province is known as the 'Egg Basket of the Philippines'?
What is the capital city of the Philippines?
Which Philippine city is officially recognized as the 'Summer Capital of the Philippines'?
In ancient Philippine mythology, what was the name of the Supreme Being who created the cosmos?
Which province is home to Mount Pinatubo, a volcano that erupted dramatically in 1991?
What is the largest lake in the Philippines by surface area?
Who was the first Spanish Governor of the Philippines, establishing the colonial government in the 1560s?
Which ancient script is considered the earliest form of writing system in the Philippines?
Who composed the Philippine national anthem 'Lupang Hinirang'?
Which province is renowned as the 'Surfing Capital of the Philippines'?
What Filipino delicacy consists of a partially formed duck embryo cooked inside its shell?
Which Philippine general led the resistance against Spanish forces at the Battle of Manila Bay in 1898 before his capture and execution?
In what year did the Philippines ratify its first constitution, which established the First Philippine Republic?
Which Philippine island served as the primary location for the filming of 'Apocalypse Now' and is known for its limestone cliffs?
In which year did the Mactan Cebu International Airport open, becoming the Philippines' second-busiest airport at the time?
Which ethnolinguistic group comprises the largest population in the Philippines and primarily inhabits the Luzon region?
What was the primary economic system that Spanish colonizers imposed on the Philippines, granting large tracts of land to the Church and favored individuals?
Which Philippine volcano erupted catastrophically in June 1991, causing the second-largest terrestrial eruption of the 20th century?
In what year did the Philippines adopt the 1987 Constitution, which established the current presidential system and term limits?
Which Philippine mountain range, located in Benguet and Mountain Province, is the longest in the archipelago and contains Mount Picoâ?
What was the name of the secret revolutionary society founded by Andrés Bonifacio in 1892 that fought for Philippine independence?
Which Philippine island group was the last to be colonized by Spain and resisted Spanish rule until the late 19th century?
What is the name of the ancient Philippine barangay system of governance that predates Spanish colonization?
Which Philippine president was the first to be elected through the EDSA People Power Revolution and served from 1986 to 1992?
In what year did the Philippines experience the EDSA People Power Revolution that ousted Ferdinand Marcos from power?
Which Philippine saint, also known as the 'Blessed Mother of the Filipinos,' was canonized in 2000?
What is the primary volcanic and tectonic characteristic of the Philippine archipelago that makes it one of the most geologically active regions?
choices: Position on the Ring of Fire

questions.q: Which Philippine general led the Filipino-American War resistance from 1899 to 1902 and later became a national hero?
Which Philippine president's regime was identified by the code name 'Maharlika' during his authoritarian rule from 1972 to 1986?
During the Battle of Mactan in 1521, which indigenous leader defeated Ferdinand Magellan's expedition?
Which Philippine island province is specifically renowned for producing high-quality marble that has been exported internationally for construction and sculpture?
Mount Apo, the highest peak in the Philippines, is located on which island?
Romblon province is internationally renowned for its production and export of which natural stone material?
Who is credited as the first President of the Philippine Republic, established in 1899?
Lapu-Lapu, a legendary Filipino warrior, is remembered for his resistance against which explorer's forces in 1521?
What event in Philippine history, occurring in 1896, is commemorated as the beginning of the revolution against Spanish rule?
Which Filipino beauty queen won the Miss Universe title in 1969?
Intramuros, the historic walled city in Manila, was established as a fortress during which colonial period?
The Puerto Princesa Subterranean River, one of the world's longest underground rivers, is located in which Philippine province?
Manuel L. Quezon served as the second President of the Commonwealth of the Philippines. In what year did he assume office?
The Tubbataha Reefs Natural Park, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, is situated in which region of the Philippines?
Cebu is historically recognized as the oldest city in the Philippines. In what year was it founded as a Spanish settlement?
In 1521, after establishing the first Spanish settlement in the Philippines, Miguel López de Legazpi founded which city as the capital of the Spanish colonial administration?
Which Filipino national hero led the Katipunan, a secret society that sparked the Philippine Revolution against Spanish rule in 1896?
The Philippine independence from the United States was officially recognized on July 4, 1946. Which Philippine president was in office at the time of this declaration?
How many islands make up the Philippines?
What is the official language of the Philippines (along with English)?

source.notes: Filipino is standardized form of Tagalog. English is co-official.

questions.q: Approximately how many years was the Philippines under Spanish rule?

source.notes: From Legazpi's arrival in 1565 to U.S. takeover in 1898.

questions.q: Which Filipino national hero authored 'Noli Me Tangere' and 'El Filibusterismo' before being executed in 1896?

source.notes: Executed by firing squad on December 30, 1896 at Bagumbayan (now Rizal Park).

questions.q: Which Filipino dictator ruled 1965-1986, declared martial law, and was ousted by the People Power Revolution?
Whose son became Philippine president in 2022 — son of the Marcos dictator?
By what name is the 1986 People Power Revolution that overthrew Marcos most commonly known?

source.notes: audit-corrected 2026-05-09: removed 'Both A and C' meta-option; recast as 'most commonly known'. Named after Epifanio de los Santos Avenue (EDSA) where the protests centered. Also called the 'Yellow Revolution' after Ninoy Aquino's yellow ribbon symbol.

questions.q: What is the most popular Filipino dish — meat braised in vinegar, soy sauce, garlic?

source.notes: Often called the 'unofficial' national dish. Chicken or pork.

questions.q: Which Filipino island is famous for its 4-km White Beach and surf?
Which Filipino boxer became an 8-division world champion?

source.notes: Pacquiao also served as a Senator.

questions.q: Which traditional Filipino vehicle is a colorful, decorated mini-bus made from reused jeeps?

source.notes: Originally made from WWII US military jeeps.
